name: Accessibility Guidelines
summary: Baseline requirements for player-facing surfaces: perceivable, operable, understandable, robust, and kind by default.

rules.statement: Every image must have alt text or be explicitly marked as decorative
rules.reasoning: Screen readers need text alternatives to convey visual content to blind users.

rules.statement: Headings must follow logical hierarchy (H1 > H2 > H3); one H1 per document
rules.reasoning: Screen readers use headings for navigation; skipping levels breaks accessibility.

rules.statement: Links must have descriptive text, never 'click here'
rules.reasoning: Screen reader users often navigate by links; generic text provides no context.
examples: Learn more about the foreman's history

rules.statement: Never rely on color alone to convey state or grouping
rules.reasoning: Color-blind users cannot distinguish color-only differences.

rules.statement: Text equivalents must be provided for all audio cues
rules.reasoning: Deaf users need text alternatives to understand audio content.
examples: [Low mechanical hum; distant footsteps on metal grating.]

rules.statement: Content warnings must be non-spoiling and placed in front matter
rules.reasoning: Content warnings help readers avoid triggering content without ruining the story.
examples: Contains: violence (industrial accident), confinement, alarm sounds

definitions.meaning: Provide text equivalents and clear structure so all users can perceive the content.
Navigation is predictable; links and anchors work across all export formats.
Language is clear; reading order makes sense; no hidden mechanics.
Content warnings and sensory safety are normal practice, not special accommodations.

heuristics.guidance: Use one sentence with concrete nouns and relations; avoid starting with 'image of...'
examples: A scarred bulkhead with spent extinguishers; a foreman's shadow reaches across the deck.

heuristics.guidance: Describe mood and affordance, not the twist or spoiler content.
heuristics.reasoning: Captions are player-facing and must maintain spoiler hygiene.

heuristics.guidance: Use role='presentation' or provide meaningful alt text; never leave alt empty without marking decorative.
heuristics.reasoning: Empty alt without decoration marking causes screen readers to announce the filename.

heuristics.guidance: Avoid startle peaks; provide fade/ramp guidance in audio notes.
heuristics.reasoning: Sudden loud sounds can harm users with hearing sensitivity or anxiety.

heuristics.guidance: Keep CW phrasing neutral with no plot reveals; list categories briefly.
examples: Contains: violence (industrial accident), confinement, alarm sounds
counter_examples: Warning: the foreman dies in an accident in chapter 3

criteria.name: Accessibility Checklist
criteria.pass_condition: All items verified: images have alt text, captions are spoiler-safe, audio has text equivalents, headings are hierarchical, links resolve in all formats, content notes present.
